title: Historical straddle reconstruction (look-ahead-audited)
acceptance_criteria: Extract the PURE straddle compute logic (straddle_value, roc, acceleration over a rolling window) out of straddle-calc.ts into a new straddle-math.ts module of pure functions. straddle-calc.ts is refactored to import and use these functions — there is ONE implementation of straddle math for live and historical.

Behaviour-preserving refactor: the existing straddle-calc.test.ts continues to pass unchanged. straddle-math.test.ts adds direct unit tests of the extracted pure functions (incl. <2 and <3 snapshot edge cases).

reconstruct-straddle.ts: for a past date range, at each cadence step determine the ATM strike from the index candle, fetch CE+PE option candles at that strike/expiry from option_ticks, compute the snapshot via straddle-math, and write straddle_snapshots tagged with the source resolution.

LOOK-AHEAD AUDIT (Critical): ATM strike and every input at step T use ONLY data at-or-before T's timestamp — never a future bar. A dedicated test asserts no future-data access (e.g. feeding a series and verifying step T's output is unchanged when T+1..N are mutated).
Fail loud (throw / explicit gap record) when a required CE or PE leg candle is absent at a step — NEVER interpolate or zero-fill.
Reads T-55's resolution/gap tags and propagates resolution onto each reconstructed snapshot so downstream consumers cannot silently mix fidelities.
All option_ticks/straddle_snapshots queries are time-range bounded.
Named exports only; TS strict; passes tsc --noEmit and lint.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
